text.en: Lay patient in the left lateral position.
Recheck BP after 5 minutes of rest; record both readings.
Call 108 — request obstetric-equipped ambulance and inform receiving facility.
Communicate to PHC/CHC: prepare loading dose of MgSO4 per FOGSI/MoHFW protocol.

rules.rationale: BP >= 160/110 mmHg in pregnancy meets the diagnostic threshold for severe pre-eclampsia and risks eclampsia/stroke; FOGSI guidelines require immediate transfer with MgSO4 ready at receiving facility.

text.en: Recheck BP after 5 minutes of rest in left lateral position.
If urine dipstick available, test for proteinuria; >= 1+ supports diagnosis.
Refer to PHC today for obstetric review and lab tests.

rules.rationale: BP >= 140/90 with at least one prodromal symptom is the FOGSI/MoHFW threshold for suspected pre-eclampsia and warrants same-day obstetric assessment.

text.en: Do NOT perform a per-vaginal examination at home.
Place patient in the left lateral position.
Note number of soaked pads; report to receiving facility.
Call 108 — request obstetric ambulance immediately.

rules.rationale: Any vaginal bleeding after 20 weeks may signal placenta praevia or abruption; both are obstetric emergencies that need facility-based diagnosis and possible operative delivery.

text.en: Ask mother to lie on left side and count movements for 1 hour; expect at least 4 movements.
If movements remain reduced, refer to PHC today for fetal heart-rate check / NST.

rules.rationale: Reduced fetal movements after 28 weeks correlate with hypoxia/IUGR; same-day fetal monitoring is required per MoHFW MH operational guidelines.

text.en: Lay the woman on her left side; do NOT put anything in the mouth.
Move sharp objects away; cushion the head.
Call 108 — eclampsia is a CRITICAL obstetric emergency.
Communicate to receiving facility: prepare MgSO4 loading dose per Dakshata protocol.

rules.rationale: Convulsions in pregnancy are presumed eclampsia until proven otherwise; mortality is high without prompt MgSO4 and operative delivery.

label.hi: गर्भावस्था में गंभीर खून की कमी

text.en: Refer to PHC/CHC for IV iron sucrose / blood transfusion as per MAA-Anaemia Mukt Bharat protocol.
Continue daily IFA and counsel on iron-rich foods.

rules.rationale: Hb < 7 g/dL is the MoHFW threshold for severe anaemia requiring institutional management to prevent peripartum cardiac failure and intra-partum haemorrhage.

label.hi: पानी फटना / समय से पहले प्रसव

text.en: Place mother in left lateral position; do NOT do internal exam.
Use a clean pad; note the colour of the fluid (clear / meconium / blood-stained).
Refer to FRU/CHC — preterm needs steroids + neonatal-care readiness.

rules.rationale: Preterm PROM and labour < 37 weeks need facility delivery, antenatal steroids, and neonatal resuscitation readiness per Dakshata.
